summary: Excellent soundboard transfer of a legendary 11/14/73 show, highlighted by a soaring Here Comes Sunshine and a marathon Truckin' → Other One → Eyes jam.
review: The Charlie Miller‑upgraded soundboard capture is crisp and well balanced, letting the band’s peak late‑1973 chemistry shine.  Reviewers rave about the second‑set marathon—Truckin’ into multiple Other Ones, Eyes of the World and a superb Wharf Rat—while the first set delivers strong renditions of Here Comes Sunshine, Cumberland Blues and China > Rider.  A few minor flubs (China Cat transition, occasional bass drop) are noted but do not detract from an overall outstanding performance.

ai_rating.rationale: 23 reviews overwhelmingly positive (16 five‑stars, 6 four‑stars, 1 three‑star) with consistent praise for both the musical performance and the high‑quality soundboard transfer.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, well‑balanced mix; Charlie Miller upgrade praised. Minor issues noted: a sloppy China Cat transition and occasional bass drop in the first set, but overall very clean.

standout_songs: Truckin' > The Other One > Eyes of the World > The Other One > Big River
China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
poor_songs: Here Comes Sunshine (ending flub noted by a few reviewers)
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set solid but occasionally pedestrian; second set a marathon of improvisation that many cite as one of the best of 1973, with seamless transitions and high energy throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for soaring lead guitar tones, exploratory solos and strong vocals; occasional slip in China Cat transition noted.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mixed feedback – many laud his ‘raging’ bass work, but a few mention moments where he was missing or less intense.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocals highlighted on Sugaree (strong) and minor lyric slip in the first verse of HCS; overall passionate performance.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith's piano described as monstrous, accurate, exploratory and a highlight throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann’s syncopated off‑beat work lauded; Billy’s drumming after Mickey’s departure called vital and career‑night level.
